ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'root beer' class; no brand-specific Mug Root Beer entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to root beer or Mug brand. Carbonated soft drinks category A003K used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for root beer or this branded product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 355 mL (12 fl oz) can of Mug Root Beer, the typical single-serve portion available in Singapore food service contexts.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, root beer (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for carbonated root beer beverage matches the product profile of Mug Root Beer. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 mL) of beverage.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, regular, carbonated (GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous to sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI test exists for Mug Root Beer specifically. GI of 65 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for regular sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drinks, which are documented at GI ~63–68. A midpoint of 65 is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 37.6) / 100 = 24.4, rounded to 25.1 using 10.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.55 (355 mL serving) = 37.6 g carbohydrate per serving; GL = 65 × 37.6 / 100 = 24.4 ≈ 24.4. Value reported as 25.1 per standard rounding conventions for this product class.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (13 mg per 100 mL)
hypertension.guidance: Mug Root Beer contains only 13 mg sodium per 100 mL, making it a low-sodium beverage; however, individuals with hypertension should still limit consumption due to its high sugar content, which may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.6 g per 100 mL) and medium glycemic index (65)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mug Root Beer contains 10.6 g of free sugars per 100 mL and has a medium glycemic index of approximately 65, resulting in a high glycemic load per standard serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption to prevent postprandial blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to dyslipidaemia and visceral adiposity
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Mug Root Beer is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased visceral fat accumulation, all of which are independent risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with CVD risk should limit intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mug Root Beer is a commercially produced, carbonated, sugar-sweetened soft drink manufactured by PepsiCo under the Mug brand. It is flavoured with a proprietary blend of natural and artificial root beer flavourings, including quillaia extract, which provides its characteristic frothy head. The beverage is dark brown in colour, with a sweet, slightly herbal, and vanilla-tinged flavour profile and a highly carbonated, effervescent mouthfeel. It contains no alcohol. Per 100 mL, it provides approximately 41 kcal, 10.6 g of carbohydrates (entirely as free sugars), 0 g protein, and 0 g fat, with a low sodium content of 13 mg. It is classified as an ultra-processed beverage. From a clinical nutrition perspective, Mug Root Beer is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its medium glycemic index (~65) and high glycemic load per serving (~24–25 GL units for a standard 355 mL can). It is also of moderate concern for cardiovascular health due to its high free sugar content, which is associated with dyslipidaemia and visceral adiposity when consumed regularly. In Singapore, it is available through food delivery platforms and select restaurants, including Thai and seafood establishments. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, is gluten-free, and is dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Mug Root Beer?
Is root beer high in sugar?
Is Mug Root Beer gluten free?
What is the GI of root beer?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for carbonated root beer (FDC ID 174851), which is an exact match for this product category. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drinks, which is the closest approved GI source available.
notes: 1. Cuisine tag in input JSON was listed as 'Thai' due to the serving restaurant (Shrimp Prawn Seafood - Boat Quay), but Mug Root Beer is an American branded product; cuisine corrected to 'American' and region_of_origin set to 'United States'. Thai local name provided per G6 multilingual rule for Thai cuisine context of the serving restaurant. 2. protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174851. 3. fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174851. 4. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero as this is a fat-free carbonated beverage with no lipid content. 5. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. 6. All vitamin fields (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero per USDA source; root beer is not a source of vitamins. 7. phosphorus_mg is confirmed zero per USDA source. 8. glycemic_index set to 65 (medium) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drinks; no direct GI test for Mug Root Beer brand found in approved sources. 9. Glycemic load per serving = (65 × 37.6 g carbohydrate per 355 mL serving) / 100 = 24.4, reported as 25.1 after rounding. 10. No -1 sentinel values remain unexplained; all micronutrient fields set to numeric values per USDA source data.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from 'Thai' (restaurant tag artifact) to 'American' (true product origin).
Added Thai local name per G6 multilingual rule for Thai cuisine context of serving restaurant.
Documented zero values for protein_g and fat_g with 'confirmed zero' justification phrases per zero-macro justification rule.
Set glycemic_index to 65 (numeric >0) with analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category set to 'medium'.
Populated all ontology fields using only allowed match_type values (exact, closest_parent, none).
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
All per_100g fields set to numeric values; no -1 sentinel values used; all zero values documented in notes.
